name: 18alpha-GA activated Nrf2-linked proteasome function in human fibroblasts
summary: Human fibroblast cell-culture evidence that Nrf2 activation can raise proteasome activity and delay senescence phenotypes.
statement: In human fibroblast cultures, 18alpha-glycyrrhetinic acid activated an Nrf2-mediated program that increased proteasome activity and content, improved survival after oxidative stress, and extended cellular replicative lifespan.
population_or_model: Human embryonic fibroblast cultures undergoing replicative senescence
time_horizon: Cell-culture passaging and acute oxidative-stress assays
quantitative_note: The abstract reports elevated proteasome activity/content, enhanced oxidative-stress survival, and lifespan extension, but this is not an organismal or clinical endpoint.
caveats: This is cell-culture evidence, not a human trial.
Nrf2 activation affects many stress-response pathways, so the finding does not isolate proteasome enhancement as the only causal mechanism.
Long-term safety and tissue-specific effects of chronic Nrf2/proteasome activation are unresolved.
